pubmed_links: [Stamberger et al. (2021) NEXMIF encephalopathy: an X-linked disorder with male and female phenotypic patterns](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33144681/); [Mooneyham et al. (2019) NEXMIF/KIDLIA knock-out mouse demonstrates autism-like behaviors, memory deficits, and impairments in synapse formation and function](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31704787/); [Campos-Sanchez et al. (2018) Novel NEXMIF pathogenic variant in a boy with severe autistic features, intellectual disability, and epilepsy, and his mildly affected mother](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/29717186/)
summary: NEXMIF is an X-linked gene whose loss-of-function variants cause a well-defined neurodevelopmental syndrome characterized by intellectual disability, autism spectrum disorder, and epilepsy, with sex-differential severity (severe in males, milder in females). Mouse knockout models recapitulate autism-like behaviors and show reduced synapse density, establishing NEXMIF as a key regulator of synapse formation and dendritic arborization. (well-established)
